title: Livestock heat and water triage
summary: Check water flow, shade, crowding, breathing, standing ability, feed access, and the most vulnerable animals first.
content: Check water flow, shade, crowding, breathing, standing ability, feed access, and the most vulnerable animals first. Contact a veterinarian or agricultural authority early when multiple animals are affected or water quality is uncertain. Do not force transport during peak heat without a safer destination and ventilation plan.
materials: Notebook or printed card. Context: “Livestock heat and water triage”.
Pencil. Context: “Livestock heat and water triage”.
Relevant labels, contacts, and official alerts — for «Livestock heat and water triage»
steps: Check water flow, shade, crowding, breathing, standing ability, feed access, and the most vulnerable animals first.

short_term: For Livestock heat and water triage, stabilize the immediate risk, communicate the decision, and schedule the next check before attention moves elsewhere.
long_term: After Livestock heat and water triage, update supplies, contact lists, maps, training, and the written plan from what actually happened.
if_method_fails: Stop Livestock heat and water triage, isolate the unsafe result, communicate the failure, and use the safest official or professionally supported fallback available.
environment_notes: Adapt Livestock heat and water triage to Greek heat, wildfire smoke, earthquakes, islands, mountain roads, apartment stairs, power cuts, water interruptions, mobility limits, and official alerts.
